purpose: Composed conformance profile: admission (signer independence) + recompute (verdict = f(controls)) + chain-fork (head_hash = sha256(content_hash|prev_head_hash)). Requested by rpelevin on autogen#7353 (2026-07-03).

vectors.note: Signer is the actor's own payment wallet -- self-approval, not an independent verdict. Fails closed on WHO signed it; the hash and recorded verdict are otherwise valid.

controls.policy=VIOLATION implies f(controls)=DENY, but the recorded verdict is ALLOW. Signer is a legitimate independent policy-issuer -- fails on WHETHER the verdict follows from its own cited inputs, not on WHO signed it.
The accepted decision committed as a receipt chain entry. Recomputing head_hash from the identical content + prior head reproduces the identical digest, deterministically.

A divergent entry at the SAME sequence position (same prior_head, different content). Produces a DIFFERENT head_hash from axis3-chain-entry-a -- both heads independently computable, so a fork is a detectable conflict, not a silent overwrite.

vectors.property: same content_hash + same prior_head -> same head_hash
vectors.note: Recomputed via a fresh call path (not reusing axis3-chain-entry-a's cached digest) -- lands on the same head_hash.

vectors.property: same sequence position + different prior_head -> detectable branch fork between two competing lineages
vectors.note: Two DIFFERENT payloads (accepted vs. verdict-not-recomputable), each independently anchored, arriving at the SAME sequence position from two DIFFERENT prior heads -- two competing lineages, not a same-lineage overwrite.

vectors.property: same payload, different chain context -> different head_hash
vectors.note: The IDENTICAL record (same content_hash) hashed under two different chain contexts (prior_head vs. prior_head_alt) must not collapse to the same head -- otherwise a valid entry from one point in history could be replayed at a different position and pass as the original commitment.

vectors.property: the verifier exposes both competing heads plus the shared sequence position as structured data, never collapses to a bare boolean
vectors.demonstrated_by: axis4b-branch-fork's shape: {sequence_position, heads: {x, y}, prior_heads: {x, y}, conflict}
vectors.note: A reviewer needs to distinguish corruption, replay, and legitimate branch divergence from the report -- a collapsed boolean cannot carry that distinction.

independent_verification.cross_checked_against: babyblueviper1/preaction-governance-conformance @ examples/composed-decision-chain-recompute/ (same construction, independently implemented, including pshkv's Axis 4 fork-matrix proposed on autogen#7353 2026-07-03)
independent_verification.result: byte-identical head_hash values across all vectors, including axis4b (position 41, heads 03d92bae.../1e1664b3..., prior_heads e3b0c442.../6b86b273...)
independent_verification.method: This repo's verify.py was written independently from the construction description (content_hash + head_hash formula, and pshkv's four-property fork-matrix description), not copied from any third party's code, then cross-checked against babyblueviper1's independently-published head_hash values. Both computations used the same source fixture (presidio-x402-decision-ref-v1.fixture.json, originally contributed to this repo by presidio-v), which is verified byte-identical between both repos.
